title: araucaria bidwillii
intro: Araucaria bidwillii, also known as the Bunya nut or Bunya pine, is a species of evergreen tree native to Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The nuts of the Araucaria bidwillii tree have been found to have health benefits, including being rich in nutrients and minerals, and have been used to treat conditions such as scurvy and anemia. The herbal preparations of Araucaria bidwillii include the use of its nuts in teas, infusions, and as a topical application for skin conditions.
